name: Robotica
definition: The realm of constructed, artificial intelligences, encompassing all forms of artificial material constructs that sense, decide and operate autonomously within the physical world.

children.name: Androids
children.definition: Robots constructed to mimic humans, in appearance, likeness and abilities.

children.name: Mechanoids
children.definition: Mechanical humanoid robots, constructed mainly from metallic or non-pliable materials.

children.name: Synthoids
children.definition: Synthetic humanoid robots virtually indistinguishable from humans in appearance, with lifelike skin made from synthetic materials.

children.name: Plastoids
children.definition: Flexible humanoid robots made with pliable materials.

children.name: Colossals
children.definition: Gigantic humanoid robots over 5 meters in size, constructed of any material.

children.name: Bionics
children.definition: Robots constructed to mimic non-human biological creatures, in appearance, likeness and abilities.

children.name: Zooids
children.definition: Biotica-inspired robots, with a likeness for existing known animals or insects, larger than 1mm in size.

children.name: Microbots
children.definition: Tiny biotica-inspired robots, with either a likeness for existing known animals or insects, or novel designforms smaller than 1mm in size.

children.name: Nanobots
children.definition: Microscopic biotica-inspired robots, with either a likeness for existing known animals or insects, or novel designforms smaller than 1µm in size.

children.name: Vessels
children.definition: Robots designed for movement, transportation and exploration, across the physical planes of land, air, water, or space.

children.name: Autons
children.definition: Vehicular robots designed to traverse and operate over land.

children.name: Drones
children.definition: Aerial robots designed to traverse and operate in the air.

children.name: Mariners
children.definition: Underwater robots designed to traverse and operate on or below water.

children.name: Rovers
children.definition: Exploration robots designed to traverse and operate underground, in space or in extraterrestrial environments.

children.name: Automata
children.definition: Stationary robots or mobile robots that are meant to operate within a single contained, fixed, or controlled environment.

children.name: Articulators
children.definition: Robotic arms, designed for precise manipulation and operation within fixed environments.

children.name: Mechatrons
children.definition: Large movable industrial robots weighing more than 200kg, designed for repetitive operation within a single fixed environment.

children.name: Terminals
children.definition: Stationary immobile robots, designed to operate within a fixed environment.

children.name: Servons
children.definition: Service-oriented robots weighing less than 200kg, designed for interactive service within fixed environments.

children.name: Automatons
children.definition: Interactive robot companions or robot toys smaller than 1 meter, designed for companionship or operation within fixed environments.

children.name: Megatech
children.definition: Massive robot megastructures over 1 million cubic meters in volume such as robot starships or planetary sized robots.

children.name: Planetoids
children.definition: Planet-shaped robots over 1 million m³ in volume.

children.name: Starships
children.definition: Large spaceship robots over 1 million m³ in volume designed to travel great distances.

children.name: Arcologies
children.definition: Very large robotic megastructures designed to either house inhabitants or to undertake exploration or scientific work well over 1 million m³ in volume.

children.name: Spectra
children.definition: Robots that challenge and transcend traditional physical boundaries, encompassing physical entities with ethereal, virtual, or shape-shifting qualities.

children.name: Virtuoids
children.definition: Virtual or holographic robot entities, within a physical housing.

children.name: Morphbots
children.definition: Shape-shifting robots.

children.name: Ethereals
children.definition: Non-conventional or energy-based robotic entities that defy the current laws of physics.
